growth_records.agit: static (no shaking reported; Balch tubes and Pyrex side-arm flasks)
growth_records.cite: Greening et al. Diverse hydrogen production and consumption pathways influence methane production in ruminants. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0464-2. [PMC6776011]
growth_records.snippet: Growth yield (OD600) 0.79±0.01; Specific growth rate (h-1) 0.58±0.19 ... Fumarate (mM) -5.5, Succinate (mM) +2.2, Acetate (mM) +21.8, Ethanol (mM) +8.7 [pure culture, 0-12 h]
growth_records.notes: Specific growth rate (mu=0.58 h-1) directly reported in Table 1 of paper (pure culture of R. albus 7, anaerobic modified Balch medium, headspace 20% CO2/80% N2, 37 degC). Uptake/secretion rates are CALCULATED as delta-concentration (Table 1 footnote: change between 0 and 12 h) divided by 12 h; these are volumetric (mM/h) not biomass-specific rates because the paper gives no OD-to-gDW conversion factor for R. albus. Fumarate: 5.5 mM/12h=0.458 mM/h; succinate: 2.2/12=0.183; acetate: 21.8/12=1.817; ethanol: 8.7/12=0.725 mM/h. Headspace H2 also increased +5.3% over 12h (gas-phase %, not concentration, so not converted to a rate here). Medium composition (Table S6) not reproduced in main text.

growth_records.agit: static (no shaking reported; Balch tubes and Pyrex side-arm flasks)
growth_records.medium: modified Balch medium (co-culture with Wolinella succinogenes DSM 1740)
growth_records.cite: Greening et al. Diverse hydrogen production and consumption pathways influence methane production in ruminants. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0464-2. [PMC6776011]
growth_records.snippet: Specific growth rate (h-1) co-culture 0.57±0.34 (Ra)/0.54±0.11 (Ws) ... Fumarate (mM) -43.1, Succinate +55.4, Acetate +32.4, Ethanol +0.3, H2 BDL [co-culture, 0-12 h]
growth_records.notes: R. albus growth rate in co-culture (mu=0.57 h-1) directly reported in Table 1, tracked by qPCR alongside W. succinogenes. Metabolite concentration changes (Table 1, 0-12h) are for the COMBINED co-culture and cannot be attributed solely to R. albus; rates calculated as delta-conc/12h: fumarate 43.1/12=3.592, succinate 55.4/12=4.617, acetate 32.4/12=2.700, ethanol 0.3/12=0.025 mM/h. H2 was below detection limit in co-culture (fully consumed). Same combined-culture metabolite rates also attached to the paired W. succinogenes co-culture record; volumetric (mM/h), not gDW-specific.

growth_records.medium: Defined anaerobic medium (Weimer defined medium, ref 45) with microcrystalline cellulose as energy source

up.method: calculated from chemostat steady state: qS = D*S/X = 0.033/h * 10.94 mM / 0.37 gDW/L

up.units: mM glucose equivalents (=1.97 g cellulose/L consumed)

yields.Yxs: 0.188 g cell DW / g glucose equivalent (cellulose)

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.method: calculated (mu = D at chemostat steady state; doubling time = ln2/0.033 = 21.0 h)
growth_records.snippet: Continuous cultures grown on cellulose consumed 1.97 g cellulose/L, equivalent to 10.94 mM glucose equivalents, and produced 11.4 mM ethanol and 14.8 mM acetate. Cell protein concentrations from cellulose ... were 0.185 ... g/L, equivalent
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, D=0.033/h, 39C, anaerobic. mu=D=0.033/h; td=ln2/0.033=21.0 h. Biomass X: cell protein 0.185 g/L, cells assumed 50% protein => DW=0.185/0.5=0.37 gDW/L (consistent with reported Yxs 0.188 g/g x 1.97 g/L consumed = 0.37 gDW/L). Specific rates via steady-state balance q=D*C/X: qGlc-equiv uptake=0.033*10.94/0.37=0.976; qEtOH=0.033*11.4/0.37=1.017; qAce=0.033*14.8/0.37=1.320 mmol/gDW/h. Substrate consumption 1.97 g/L measured gravimetrically as acid-detergent fiber; products by chemical analysis. Paper states R. albus 7 produces H2 and CO2 from cellulose (cited ref 29) but H2/CO2 rates NOT quantified here. pH not explicitly stated.

growth_records.medium: Defined anaerobic medium (Weimer defined medium, ref 45) with cellobiose as energy source

up.method: calculated: qS = D*S/X = 0.033/h * 25.67 mM glucose-equiv / 0.63 gDW/L
calculated: 25.67 mM glucose-equiv / 2 = 12.84 mM cellobiose; qS = 0.033*12.84/0.63

up.units: mM glucose equivalents (=4.62 g glucose-equiv/L consumed)

yields.Yxs: 0.136 g cell DW / g glucose equivalent (cellobiose)

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.method: calculated (mu = D at chemostat steady state; doubling time = ln2/0.033 = 21.0 h)
growth_records.snippet: Continuous cultures grown on cellobiose consumed 4.62 g glucose equivalents/L (25.67 mM) and produced 26.9 mM ethanol and 27.7 mM acetate. Cell protein concentrations from ... cellobiose cultures were ... 0.315 g/L, equivalent to cell yield
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, D=0.033/h, 39C, anaerobic. mu=D=0.033/h; td=21.0 h. X: cell protein 0.315 g/L, 50% protein => DW=0.315/0.5=0.63 gDW/L (consistent: Yxs 0.136 g/g x 4.62 g/L = 0.628 gDW/L). Substrate consumption reported as glucose equivalents (25.67 mM); cellobiose molarity = 12.84 mM. Specific rates via q=D*C/X: qGlc-equiv uptake=0.033*25.67/0.63=1.345; qEtOH=0.033*26.9/0.63=1.409; qAce=0.033*27.7/0.63=1.451 mmol/gDW/h. H2/CO2 not quantified.

growth_records.medium: Defined anaerobic medium (ref 45) supplemented with microcrystalline cellulose

sec.units: mmol/g dry polysaccharide substrate (endpoint yield; SD 0.20)
mmol/g dry polysaccharide substrate (endpoint yield; SD 0.16)

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Cellulose - Ethanol 3.89 +/- 0.20, Acetate 2.33 +/- 0.16 mmol/g dry polysaccharide substrate. Reducing sugars 0.3 +/- 0.5%.
growth_records.notes: Batch, 39C, 72 h, no shaking, anaerobic. Values are endpoint product YIELDS (mmol product / g dry substrate), NOT specific rates; no growth rate or time-course reported so mu and qS not derivable. Substrate = crystalline cellulose (Sigmacell). H2/CO2 produced (per text) but not quantified.

sec.units: mmol/g dry polysaccharide substrate (endpoint yield; SD 0.19)
mmol/g dry polysaccharide substrate (endpoint yield; SD 0.19)

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Soy polysaccharides - Ethanol 1.22 +/- 0.19, Acetate 0.93 +/- 0.19 mmol/g dry polysaccharide substrate. Reducing sugars 15.2 +/- 4.2%.
growth_records.notes: Batch, 39C, 72 h, anaerobic. Lowest fermentation-product yield among fermented substrates. Endpoint yields per g substrate, not rates. Citrus pectin and inulin were hydrolyzed to reducing sugars but NOT fermented (no ethanol/acetate) so no records made for them. Resistant starch, Type II arabinogalactan, curdlan, laminarin were neither hydrolyzed nor utilized.
